refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (vehicle control, teleoperation, safety constraints) to enable proper decomposition and allocation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, standard earthmoving scenario). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) against productivity_target. Cross-check against safety incident log and worksite policy compliance audit.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving operations (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time ≤ target_cycle_time to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or teleoperation) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to enable safe, efficient task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle count) to enable real-time performance tracking.

FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (speed limits, geofence, emergency stop, operator certification) that do not degrade productivity below productivity_target under normal operations.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-performance hydraulic drivetrain and bucket control system with optimized cycle-time logic; couple with real-time telemetry and safety interlock middleware.

DP-B: Decompose productivity into vehicle performance (DP-B1: optimized hydraulics, bucket geometry, load capacity) and operator effectiveness (DP-B2: low-latency teleoperation + situational awareness UI + safety guardrails).
DP-C: Implement modular task execution framework (dig, load, transport, dump) with parameterized cycle timing; integrate safety policy engine and productivity telemetry pipeline.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Decompose productivity into vehicle performance subsystem (optimized hydraulics, bucket geometry, load capacity, cycle-time control) and operator effectiveness subsystem (low-latency teleoperation interface, situational awareness telemetry, safety guardrails enforcement). Couple via shared telemetry and tasking interfaces.

potential_sources: Telemetry latency: vehicle performance metrics must reach operator interface with <500ms latency to enable responsive control.
Safety interlock coupling: vehicle performance limits (e.g., max speed, bucket angle) must be enforced by safety services, not duplicated in vehicle or teleoperation modules.
Productivity measurement: cycle-time and output metrics must be consistently computed from shared vehicle state (position, bucket load, cycle phase).
coupling_check.notes: DP-B avoids monolithic coupling by explicitly separating vehicle capability from operator effectiveness, with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert). Coupling is managed via middleware (Safety Services, Telemetry pipeline).

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle hydraulic system can achieve target cycle time without exceeding safety limits (pressure, temperature, load).
Assumption: Teleoperation network link provides <500ms round-trip latency under representative site conditions.
Risk: If vehicle cycle time cannot meet productivity_target due to hydraulic constraints, DP-B cannot be realized; may require hardware redesign (DP revision).
Risk: If teleoperation latency exceeds 500ms, operator effectiveness degrades, reducing actual productivity below target despite vehicle capability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: None at this level. DP-B is sound and decomposable. Proceed to sub-level decomposition of vehicle performance and operator effectiveness subsystems.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration boundary encompassing vehicle, teleoperation, safety services, and telemetry/tasking middleware. Owns end-to-end productivity KPI realization and safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle state (position, bucket load, cycle phase, hydraulic pressure, temperature) streamed to operator interface and safety services for monitoring and control.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator commands (dig, load, transport, dump, emergency stop) received from on-site or remote operator interface.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety constraint violations (geofence breach, overspeed, overload, emergency stop trigger) broadcast to operator and safety services.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone for teleoperation, telemetry, and safety alert exchange between vehicle, operator interface, and safety services.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary. CR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI) is inherently a system-level requirement. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns hydraulic drivetrain, bucket control, and cycle-time execution (FR-NGHE-001.1). However, productivity KPI also depends on operator effectiveness (FR-NGHE-001.2) and safety constraints (FR-NGHE-001.4), which are not contained in Vehicle. Significant diffusion: Vehicle must interface with Teleoperation (Tasking input) and Safety Services (SafetyAlert output, constraint enforcement).

Teleoperation owns operator interface and situational awareness (FR-NGHE-001.2). Productivity also depends on vehicle performance (FR-NGHE-001.1) and safety constraints (FR-NGHE-001.4), which are external. High diffusion: Teleoperation depends on Telemetry (vehicle state), Tasking (operator input), SafetyAlert (constraint feedback), and Network Link.

Safety Services owns safety constraint enforcement (FR-NGHE-001.4). Productivity KPI also depends on vehicle performance and operator effectiveness, which are external. Very high diffusion: Safety Services must monitor Telemetry, enforce constraints on Vehicle via Tasking/SafetyAlert, and coordinate with Teleoperation.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: CR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI) is a system-level requirement that cannot be fully contained within any single sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). It requires orchestration across all three subsystems plus telemetry and tasking middleware. Therefore, NGHE Solution (top-level system boundary) is the correct IM assignment. Sub-level decomposition will allocate specific aspects (vehicle cycle time, operator responsiveness, safety constraint impact) to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services respectively.
containment_risks: Risk: If Vehicle module cannot achieve target cycle time due to hydraulic constraints, productivity KPI cannot be met regardless of operator effectiveness or safety compliance. Mitigation: Validate vehicle cycle-time capability early (prototype testing, simulation).

Risk: If Teleoperation latency exceeds acceptable threshold, operator effectiveness degrades, reducing actual productivity. Mitigation: Establish latency SLA (e.g., <500ms round-trip) and validate Network Link performance.

Risk: If Safety Services constraints are too restrictive (e.g., overly conservative speed limits), they may artificially cap productivity below target. Mitigation: Tune safety constraints in collaboration with Safety Manager and Site Manager to balance safety and productivity.

Risk: Productivity measurement (FR-NGHE-001.3) requires consistent telemetry from Vehicle and accurate cycle-phase detection. If telemetry is unreliable or cycle-phase logic is ambiguous, productivity metrics will be inaccurate. Mitigation: Define clear cycle-phase state machine and validate telemetry accuracy.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision needed. NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for CR-NGHE-001. Proceed to sub-level decomposition to allocate specific aspects to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (decompose productivity into vehicle performance and operator effectiveness subsystems) directly addresses CR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI). The decomposition is logically sound: vehicle performance determines cycle time, operator effectiveness determines task execution quality and safety compliance, and both are necessary and sufficient to achieve productivity_target. No independence violations detected.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry latency: Vehicle must stream state to Teleoperation with <500ms latency. If Network Link is congested or unreliable, latency may exceed threshold, degrading operator responsiveness and productivity.

Safety constraint coupling: Vehicle performance limits (speed, bucket angle, load) must be enforced by Safety Services, not duplicated in Vehicle or Teleoperation. Inconsistent enforcement could lead to safety violations or productivity loss.

Productivity measurement: Cycle-time and output metrics must be consistently computed from shared vehicle state. If telemetry is incomplete or cycle-phase detection is ambiguous, metrics will be inaccurate.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because it encompasses all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). Productivity KPI realization requires orchestration across all subsystems; no single sub-module can be assigned ownership. Diffusion is managed via well-defined interfaces and middleware (telemetry pipeline, safety interlock).

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: CR-NGHE-001 is verifiable via representative operations trial: measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) over agreed period and compare against productivity_target. Verification requires instrumentation of Vehicle (cycle-time sensors, load sensors) and Teleoperation (operator input logging) to enable post-trial analysis.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution (top-level system). Sub-level decomposition will allocate specific aspects: FR-NGHE-001.1 (vehicle cycle time) to Vehicle module, FR-NGHE-001.2 (operator effectiveness) to Teleoperation module, FR-NGHE-001.3 (productivity metrics) to telemetry/tasking middleware, FR-NGHE-001.4 (safety constraints) to Safety Services module. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Establish latency SLA for Telemetry and Tasking interfaces (e.g., <500ms round-trip) to ensure operator responsiveness.
Define cycle-phase state machine (dig, load, transport, dump, idle) to enable consistent productivity measurement.
Establish safety constraint tuning process (collaboration between Safety Manager, Site Manager, and System Engineer) to balance safety and productivity.
Plan early validation of Vehicle cycle-time capability (prototype testing, simulation) to de-risk DP realization.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level system KPI that requires decomposition into sub-FRs (vehicle performance, operator effectiveness, safety constraints, productivity measurement). DP-B is sound and decomposable. NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary. All gates pass. Proceed to sub-level functional decomposition to allocate specific aspects to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and telemetry/tasking middleware.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving operations (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time ≤ target_cycle_time to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or teleoperation) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to enable safe, efficient task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle count) to enable real-time performance tracking.

FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (speed limits, geofence, emergency stop, operator certification) that do not degrade productivity below productivity_target under normal operations.

assumptions: Vehicle hydraulic system can achieve target cycle time without exceeding safety limits (pressure, temperature, load).
Teleoperation network link provides <500ms round-trip latency under representative site conditions.
Safety constraints can be tuned to balance safety and productivity without artificial caps on vehicle performance.
Productivity measurement can be accurately computed from vehicle telemetry (cycle-phase detection, load sensors, position sensors).
Operator (on-site or remote) has sufficient training and situational awareness to execute tasks safely and efficiently.
open_risks: Vehicle cycle-time capability: If hydraulic system cannot meet target cycle time, productivity KPI cannot be achieved. Mitigation: Early prototype testing and simulation.
Teleoperation latency: If Network Link latency exceeds 500ms, operator responsiveness degrades, reducing actual productivity. Mitigation: Network performance validation and SLA enforcement.

Safety constraint tuning: If constraints are too restrictive, they artificially cap productivity. If too permissive, they compromise safety. Mitigation: Collaborative tuning process with Safety Manager and Site Manager.

Productivity measurement accuracy: If telemetry is unreliable or cycle-phase detection is ambiguous, metrics will be inaccurate. Mitigation: Validation of telemetry accuracy and cycle-phase state machine.

Operator effectiveness: If operator lacks training or situational awareness, actual productivity may fall below target despite vehicle capability. Mitigation: Operator training program and situational awareness UI design.
evidence_needed: Vehicle cycle-time capability data (prototype testing, simulation results, hydraulic system specifications).
Network Link latency measurements under representative site conditions (field testing, network simulation).
Safety constraint tuning rationale (collaboration notes, safety analysis, productivity impact assessment).
Telemetry accuracy validation (sensor calibration, cycle-phase state machine verification).
Operator training and situational awareness UI design (usability testing, operator feedback).
Representative operations trial results (productivity measurements, safety incident log, policy compliance audit).
